description: Server-side client token generation for v6 SDK initialization

v5_pattern.authentication: Client ID exposed in script URL
v5_pattern.security_issue: Client credentials visible in browser source

v6_pattern.security_improvement: Client secret never exposed to browser
v6_pattern.expiration: As specified by PayPal API response
v6_pattern.refresh_strategy: Generate new token when expired or near expiration

required_server_endpoint.description: Generate and return browser-safe client token

security_requirements: NEVER expose PAYPAL_CLIENT_SECRET in frontend code
ALWAYS use environment variables for credentials
ALWAYS use HTTPS for all API communication
ALWAYS validate token expiration before use
ALWAYS check response content-type before parsing JSON
ALWAYS log PayPal debug IDs for troubleshooting

server_side.duration: Cache with appropriate buffer time for safety

client_side.alternative: Request new token on each page load

authentication_failure.resolution: Verify credentials in PayPal Developer Dashboard

network_error.resolution: Implement retry logic with exponential backoff

token_lifecycle.generation: Server generates token via PayPal OAuth API
token_lifecycle.delivery: Server sends token to client via endpoint
token_lifecycle.expiration: As specified by PayPal API response
token_lifecycle.refresh: Client requests new token after expiration
token_lifecycle.revocation: Automatic after expiration (no manual revocation needed)

required.name: PAYPAL_CLIENT_ID
required.source: PayPal Developer Dashboard → My Apps & Credentials

required.name: PAYPAL_CLIENT_SECRET
required.source: PayPal Developer Dashboard → My Apps & Credentials

upgrade_checklist: Create environment variables for PayPal credentials
Implement OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow on server
Add proper error handling with PayPal debug IDs
Test token generation in sandbox environment
Verify tokens work with SDK initialization
Update frontend to fetch token from server
Remove client-id from v5 script URLs
Test token expiration and refresh logic

common_mistakes.impact: JSON parsing errors when server returns HTML error page
common_mistakes.fix: Validate content-type header before parsing JSON

common_mistakes.impact: Excessive API calls to PayPal, potential rate limiting
common_mistakes.fix: Cache tokens for 13-14 minutes on server
Use separate credentials for sandbox and production

common_mistakes.impact: Difficult to troubleshoot errors with PayPal support
common_mistakes.fix: Always extract and log debug_id from error responses

testing_checklist: Test token generation with valid credentials
Test token generation with invalid credentials (should fail gracefully)
Test token expiration and refresh logic
Test error handling for network failures
Test SDK initialization with generated token
Verify no credentials exposed in browser network tab
Test in both sandbox and production environments
Verify proper error messages shown to users
